song.title: O the Bitter Shame and Sorrow
song.lyrics:
[V1] 1) O, the bitter shame and sorrow, That a time could ever be, When I let the Savior’s pity Plead in vain, and proudly answered, “All of self, and none of Thee, All of self, and none of Thee.”
[V2] 2) Yet He found me; I beheld Him Bleeding on the cursèd tree; Heard Him pray, “Forgive them, Father!” And my wistful heart said faintly, “Some of self, and some of Thee, Some of self, and some of Thee.”
[V3] 3) Day by day His tender mercy, Healing, helping, full and free, Sweet and strong, and ah! so patient, Brought me lower while I whispered, “Less of self, and more of Thee, Less of self, and more of Thee.”
[V4] 4) Higher than the highest heavens, Deeper than the deepest sea, Lord, Thy love at last hath conquered; Grant me now my heart’s petition, “None of self, and all of Thee, None of self, and all of Thee.”
song.author: Theodore Monod
